source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 2.56 an iron needle 1 cm long and 0.1 cm in diameter is placed in a uniform magnetic field of $H_{0}=1000 \mathrm{Gs}$ with its long axis along the field direction. Give an approximate formula for $\mathbf{H}(\mathbf{r})$ valid for distances $r \gg 1 \mathrm{~cm}$. Here $r$ is measured from the center of the needle as origin. (Note: the saturation value of $\mathbf{B}$ in iron is approximately 2000 Gs.)

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 2.56 an iron needle 1 cm long and 0.1 cm in diameter is placed in a uniform magnetic field of $H_{0}=1000 \mathrm{Gs}$ with its long axis along the field direction. Give an approximate formula for $\mathbf{H}(\mathbf{r})$ valid for distances $r \gg 1 \mathrm{~cm}$. Here $r$ is measured from the center of the needle as origin. (Note: the saturation value of $\mathbf{B}$ in iron is approximately 2000 Gs.)
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

metadata.reasoning_chain: For distances $r \gg 1 \mathrm{~cm}$ the iron needle can be treated as a magnetic dipole with moment $\mathbf{m}$. Take the $z$-axis along the axis of the needle. Write $\mathbf{H}_{\text{ext}}=H_{0}\mathbf{e}_{z}$, $\mathbf{m}=m\mathbf{e}_{z}$. In Gaussian units the magnetic field at position $\mathbf{r}$ is approximately $\mathbf{H}(\mathbf{r})=\mathbf{H}_{0}-\nabla\left(\frac{\mathbf{m}\cdot\mathbf{r}}{r^{3}}\right)=\mathbf{H}_{0}-\frac{\mathbf{m}}{r^{3}}+\frac{3(\mathbf{m}\cdot\mathbf{r})\mathbf{r}}{r^{5}}$. As the magnetic field due to the iron needle is much weaker than the external field $H_{0}$, $m/r^{3}\ll H_{0}$. As the tangential component of $\mathbf{H}$ is continuous at the boundary, the magnetic field within the iron needle may be taken as approximately $\mathbf{H}_{\mathrm{in}}=\mathbf{H}_{0}$. The magnetization of the needle is then $\mathbf{M}=\frac{1}{4\pi}\left(\mathbf{B}_{\text{in}}-\mathbf{H}_{\text{in}}\right)$. With the volume of the needle equal to $V=(0.05)^{2}\pi\,\mathrm{cm}^{3}$, $B_{\text{in}}=2\times10^{4}\mathrm{Gs}$, $H_{\text{in}}\approx H_{0}=10^{3}\mathrm{Gs}$, the magnetic moment of the needle is $m=V\,M=\frac{(0.05)^{2}\pi}{4\pi}\times(2-0.1)\times10^{4}=11.9\,\mathrm{Gs}\cdot\mathrm{cm}^{3}$. In polar coordinates the magnetic field at distances $r \gg 1 \mathrm{~cm}$ has components $H_{r}=H_{0}\cos\theta+2m\frac{\cos\theta}{r^{3}}=(1000+\frac{23.8}{r^{3}})\cos\theta\,\mathrm{Gs}$ and $H_{\theta}=-H_{0}\cos\theta+\frac{m\sin\theta}{r^{3}}=(-1000+\frac{11.9}{r^{3}})\sin\theta\,\mathrm{Gs}$.
